    M = grid.rows
    N = grid.cols
    
    # Max possible XOR value is 1023 (2^10 - 1). We need 1024 slots (0 to 1023).
    MAX_XOR = 1024

    # DP table: dp[i][j] stores a boolean array of size MAX_XOR.
    # dp[i][j][s] is true if XOR sum s is achievable at (i, j).
    
    dp = Array(M) { |i|
        Array(N) { |j|
            # Initialize the boolean array representing the set of reachable XOR sums
            Array(MAX_XOR) { |k| false }
        }
    }

    # Base Case: (0, 0)
    start_xor = grid[0][0].to_i
    dp[0][0][start_xor] = true

    # Fill the DP table
    for i in 0...M
        for j in 0...N
            if i == 0 and j == 0
                next
            end

            current_value = grid[i][j].to_i

require "set"

def solve(grid : Array(Array(Int64))) : Int64
  m = grid.size
  n = grid[0].size
  
  # dp[i][j] stores the Set of reachable XOR sums at (i, j)
  # Since m*n <= 1000, this DP approach is feasible.
  dp = Array(Array(Set(Int64))) { m }
  
  # Initialize DP table structure
  m.each_with_index do |_, i|
    dp[i] = Array(Set(Int64)) { n }
  end
  
  # Base case (0, 0)
  dp[0][0] = Set(Int64) { grid[0][0] }
  
  # Iterate through the grid row by row, column by column
  (0...m).each do |i|
    (0...n).each do |j|
      if i == 0 && j == 0
        next
      end
      
      current_cell_value = grid[i][j]
      current_set = Set(Int64)()
      
      # 1. Check path from above (i-1, j)
      if i > 0
        prev_set = dp[i - 1][j]
        prev_set.each do |xor_sum|
          current_set.add(xor_sum ^ current_cell_value)
        end
      end
      
      # 2. Check path from left (i, j-1)
      if j > 0
        prev_set = dp[i][j - 1]
        prev_set.each do |xor_sum|
          current_set.add(xor_sum ^ current_cell_value)
        end
      end
      
      dp[i][j] = current_set
    end
  end
  
  # The result is the minimum value in DP[m-1][n-1]
  final_set = dp[m - 1][n - 1]
  
  # Since m, n >= 1, the final set is guaranteed to be non-empty
  final_set.min
end

#include <Array>

# Define the maximum possible XOR state size. Since max grid value is 1023 (2^10 - 1), 1024 states are enough.
XOR_SIZE = 1024

def solve(grid : Array(Array(Int64))) : Int64
  M = grid.rows
  N = grid.cols

  if M == 0 || N == 0
    return 0 # Should not happen based on constraints
  end

  # dp[i][j] is an Array[Bool] of size XOR_SIZE. 
  # dp[i][j][k] is true if XOR sum k is achievable at (i, j).
  # We use Array(Int) for indices and booleans for state.
  dp = Array(M) { |i|
    Array(N) { |j|
      Array(XOR_SIZE) { false }
    }
  }

  # Base Case (0, 0)
  val00 = grid[0][0]
  # We cast val00 to Int for array indexing since it's <= 1023
  dp[0][0][val00.to_i] = true

  # 1. Initialize first row (i = 0)
  for j in 1...N
    val_new = grid[0][j].to_i
    for k in 0...XOR_SIZE
      if dp[0][j-1][k]
        new_xor = k ^ val_new
        dp[0][j][new_xor] = true
      end
    end
  end

  # 2. Initialize first column (j = 0)
  for i in 1...M
    val_new = grid[i][0].to_i
    for k in 0...XOR_SIZE
      if dp[i-1][0][k]
        new_xor = k ^ val_new
        dp[i][0][new_xor] = true
      end
    end
  end

  # 3. Fill the rest of the DP table
  for i in 1...M
    for j in 1...N
      val_new = grid[i][j].to_i
      
      # Check paths coming from UP (i-1, j)
      for k in 0...XOR_SIZE
        if dp[i-1][j][k]
          new_xor = k ^ val_new
          dp[i][j][new_xor] = true
        end
      end

      # Check paths coming from LEFT (i, j-1)
      for k in 0...XOR_SIZE
        if dp[i][j-1][k]
          new_xor = k ^ val_new
          dp[i][j][new_xor] = true
        end
      end
    end
  end

  # 4. Find the minimum achievable XOR sum at (M-1, N-1)
  min_xor = XOR_SIZE 

  for k in 0...XOR_SIZE
    if dp[M-1][N-1][k]
      min_xor = k
      break # Since we iterate upwards from 0, the first one found is the minimum
    end
  end

  return min_xor.to_int64
end
